notes.eibor: 3.900% as of July 10 2026 (CBUAE tool web fallback); CBUAE base rate 3.65% confirmed July 13; EIBOR-base spread 25bps (within normal 10-30bps range); 3 consecutive unchanged days (streak began July 10); estimated unchanged July 13
notes.brent: July 13 07:02 UTC: $79.20 midpoint (TradingEconomics $78.93 / BrentWatch $79.49); FRED July 6 close $69.56 stale — overridden; +$9.64/bbl vs pre-escalation close; 4th consecutive rising session since July 8 ceasefire collapse
notes.wti: July 13 web-sourced $74.51 (ConvexTrade/FXLeaders); FRED July 6 close $69.60 stale — overridden
notes.hormuz: CONTINUING - Day 6 of active military exchange since July 8 ceasefire collapse. Iran claims Strait closed 'until further notice'; US CENTCOM formally denies closure and reports additional strikes. Brent $79.20 — at upper bound of partial disruption scenario ($72-80). Scenarios: Partial/temporary (35%, $72-80), Sustained 2-4 weeks (40%, $85-100), Full escalation (25%, $100-120+). ADNOC Fujairah pipeline (1.5 mb/d) provides ~50% UAE export bypass coverage.
notes.uae_opec_exit: UAE formally exited OPEC+ effective May 1 2026. UAE production reached 3.8 mb/d in June 2026 (Reuters July 6 2026) — near-record highs. UAE targeting 5 mb/d under We the UAE 2031. No longer quota-constrained.
notes.make_it_in_emirates: AED 180bn industrial procurement opportunities announced at Make it in the Emirates 2026. Localisation of 5,000+ products. Largest single Operation 300bn accelerant on record. If 20% converts to incremental industrial GDP over 5 years: AED 7.2bn/year additional pace — closes the AED 6.6bn/yr pace deficit.
notes.fed: June 17 FOMC (5th consecutive hold; Warsh inaugural; unanimous 12-0); dot plot median 3.8% implies one hike; FOMC minutes July 10 show officials deeply divided (half support hike by year-end); 25% probability July 29 hike; June CPI due July 14 — CRITICAL; FEDFUNDS 3.63% (June, 42 days stale — assumed unchanged); next FOMC July 29 2026
notes.cbuae: 3.65% confirmed July 13 2026; 214 consecutive unchanged days; EIBOR 3M 3.900% (July 10 est.); peg intact
notes.us_cpi: May 2026: 4.2% YoY (last known); June 2026 data due July 14 (TOMORROW) — CRITICAL WATCH; FRED CPIAUCSL stale 73 days
notes.us_10yr: 4.54% (FRED July 9, valid, -2bps from 4.56% July 8); breakeven inflation 2.24% (FRED July 10, valid, +1bp from 2.23%); real yield ~2.30%
notes.opec_plus: OPEC basket price unavailable (scraper null); UAE exited OPEC+ May 2026; UAE production 3.8 mb/d (Reuters July 6 2026)
notes.operation_300bn: AED 200bn milestone confirmed May 4 2026; 40.1% of gap closed [(200-133)/(300-133)]; AED 100bn remaining; AED 20.0bn/yr pace required; current pace AED 13.4bn/yr; AED 6.6bn/yr acceleration needed; at current pace ~2034 (3 years late); with Make it in Emirates 20% conversion: AED 20.6bn/yr pace, on track for 2031; fiscal buffer $24.20/bbl at $79.20 Brent; AED 123.4bn/yr fiscal surplus above breakeven
notes.fred_status: FEDFUNDS 3.63% (June, 42 days stale — assumed unchanged); INDPRO 102.65 (May, 73 days stale — background only); DCOILBRENTEU $69.56 (July 6, 7 days stale — overridden by web $79.20 July 13); DCOILWTICO $69.60 (July 6, 7 days stale — web override $74.51 July 13); DGS10 4.54% (July 9, valid); T10YIE 2.24% (July 10, valid); CPIAUCSL May 2026 (73 days stale — June data due July 14)
notes.petrochemical: AED 1,278mn/yr additional feedstock cost at $79.20 Brent (+$9.64/bbl surge); net manufacturing headwind ~AED 960mn/yr after ADNOC offset; 60% pass-through rate applied to AED 17.5bn feedstock base

claude_md_patches_applied.patch: AED/USD peg transmission chain must appear as one explicit connected arrow-chain sentence in every brief. Use literal form: Fed [action] at X.XX% → CBUAE mirrors at X.XX% → EIBOR 3M at X.XX% → EDB floating-rate portfolio at EIBOR+200bps = X.XX% → SME quarterly debt service [impact]. Non-droppable element of Type A on every run.

Tag every inline use of estimated EIBOR with (est.) directly next to the figure in every scenario line of the calculation block, not just once at the top. EIBOR source line must be first line of calculation block.
EIBOR estimation basis must appear as first line of calculation code block. Sources section mandatory with vintage dates. Keep brief body under 3,500 words.
Use **Key number:** (bold, lowercase) and **Watch list — next 72 hours:** (bold). Sources and Methodology mandatory. No YAML frontmatter.
EIBOR estimation chain must be first line of calculation block. Stale FRED series disclosed by name with vintage dates in body AND Methodology. Methodology section mandatory.
Always run all four required calculations in every brief. Corrected Op300bn method: (200-133)/(300-133)=40.1% of gap, not 200/300=66.7%.
